task_completion.description: Open the dwanko_run.tnz scene in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenToonz application is open and the window title, Xsheet, or timeline displays 'dwanko_run'.

task_completion.description: Apply a Negative or Invert color effect to the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX Schematic or Xsheet shows an 'Invert' or 'Negative' effect node connected to the camera/scene, OR the main viewer shows the animation with inverted colors (e.g., a black background instead of white).

task_completion.description: Set the render output directory to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window shows the 'Save In' or output folder path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/negative_fx' or '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/negative_fx/'.

task_completion.description: Set the render output format to PNG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window shows the file format dropdown or extension set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Set the render frame range to at least 12 frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window shows the 'To' frame set to 12 or higher, ensuring at least 12 frames will be rendered.

task_completion.description: Render the sequence to the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A render progress bar is visible in OpenToonz, OR a file manager/terminal shows multiple PNG files generated in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/negative_fx' directory with inverted colors visible in the thumbnails.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenToonz graphical interface (menus, schematic, output settings) rather than using terminal commands to bypass the software.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external tools to invert the colors.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inversion effect was applied inside OpenToonz. There is no evidence of the agent using ImageMagick, GIMP, Python scripts, or other external image editors to invert the frames after a normal render.

integrity.description: The agent actually rendered the scene from OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicked the Render button or used the Render menu in OpenToonz, rather than copying pre-existing files into the output directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Source scene: /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. Output directory: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/negative_fx. Minimum frame count: 12. Output format: PNG. The rendered images must not be solid colors (standard deviation > 10), meaning the negative effect must be applied to the actual animation, not a blank frame.
